hook: You already know how to measure things and work with numbers — geometry puts those skills to work in the world around you. But how do architects design buildings, how do game developers build virtual worlds, and how do GPS systems pinpoint your location? This unit unlocks the language of shape, space, and position that makes all of that possible.
concept: Every topic in this unit builds your ability to describe, classify, and measure the visual world using precise mathematical tools — together, these ideas form the foundation of geometry, the study of shapes, space, and their properties.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to identify and name the three fundamental building blocks of all geometry — points, lines, and planes — and understand how they relate to one another.
Master how to measure and classify angles as acute, right, obtuse, or straight based on their degree measures, such as recognizing that a $90°$ angle forms a perfect corner.
Apply the rules of complementary ($90°$ sum), supplementary ($180°$ sum), and vertical angles to find missing angle measures in diagrams without measuring.
You will learn to sort triangles by their side lengths (scalene, isosceles, equilateral) and by their angles (acute, right, obtuse) to precisely describe any triangle you encounter.
Master identifying and naming polygons — from triangles to decagons — by counting sides and recognizing whether they are regular or irregular.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Perimeter and Area of Basic Shapes
topic_roadmap.description: You will calculate the perimeter and area of common shapes like rectangles and triangles, connecting formulas such as $A =  rac{1}{2}bh$ to real-world situations like flooring a room or fencing a yard.
You will learn to plot and identify points using ordered pairs $(x, y)$ on the coordinate plane, laying the groundwork for graphing equations and understanding maps and data.
